steps.title: Understanding percentage increase
steps.content:
When a quantity increases by 7%, the new value equals the original amount plus 7% of the original. Mathematically:
$$\text{New} = \text{Original} + 0.07 \times \text{Original}$$
Factoring out the original:
$$\text{New} = \text{Original}(1 + 0.07) = 1.07 \times \text{Original}$$

steps.title: Finding the multiplier k
steps.content:
From our calculation, the 2016 population equals 1.07 times the 2015 population. We're told the 2016 population equals k times the 2015 population. Therefore:
$$k = 1.07$$
That is the correct answer.
